name: Interlochen Arts Academy
about: Interlochen Arts Academy is an independent, coeducational arts boarding high school in Interlochen, Michigan, affiliated with Interlochen Center for the Arts. Opened in 1962, it was the first arts boarding high school of its kind in the United States, combining college-preparatory academics with conservatory-caliber training in music, dance, theatre, visual arts, creative writing, film and new media, and interdisciplinary arts. The school sits on a roughly 1,200-acre campus between two lakes in northern Michigan.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend colleges and conservatories including The Juilliard School, Curtis Institute of Music, Eastman School of Music, Harvard University, Princeton University, and Rhode Island School of Design; about 82% enroll at a top-50 college.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States)
notable_alumni: Josh Groban, Norah Jones, Sufjan Stevens, Terry Crews, Ben Foster, Felicity Huffman, Ed Helms
financial_aid_notes: About 83% of students receive merit scholarships and need-based aid; the school awards roughly $17.5 million in financial aid annually.
head_of_school: Trey Devey (President, Interlochen Center for the Arts)

name: The Leelanau School
about: The Leelanau School is a small coeducational, independent college-preparatory boarding and day school in Glen Arbor, Michigan, set on a 42-acre wooded campus on the shore of Lake Michigan. Founded in 1929, it serves students in grades 9-12 with an emphasis on individualized, experiential learning and support for students with learning differences and ADHD. The intentionally small enrollment allows for small classes and close student-teacher relationships.
financial_aid_notes: Approximately one-third of students receive financial aid.

name: Cotter High School
about: Cotter High School is part of Cotter Schools, a Roman Catholic, coeducational day and boarding school in Winona, Minnesota, and the city's only Catholic school. Founded in 1911, it offers boarding for students in grades 7-12 and enrolls students from the Winona area and from more than a dozen countries. The school competes athletically as the Cotter Ramblers.
accreditation: North Central Association of Colleges and Schools
notable_alumni: Mike Leaf (basketball coach), Grace Ping (distance runner)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid and tuition assistance are available; international all-inclusive boarding tuition is approximately $55,000.

name: St. Croix Lutheran High School
about: St. Croix Lutheran Academy (operating as St. Croix Lutheran High School at the secondary level) is a private Lutheran coeducational middle and high school in West St. Paul, Minnesota, affiliated with the Wisconsin Evangelical Lutheran Synod. Founded in 1958, it serves roughly 500 students in grades 6-12, including about 150 boarding students from across the United States and around 15 countries. Its international program, begun in 1995, has long made up 20% or more of the student body.
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid, scholarships, and tuition assistance are available to domestic families; international students are not eligible for tuition assistance. International boarding tuition is approximately $24,300 plus a $9,500 residence fee.

name: Missouri Military Academy
about: Missouri Military Academy is an all-boys, independent college-preparatory military boarding school in Mexico, Missouri, founded in 1889. It serves grades 7-12 on a 288-acre campus and operates as a U.S. Army JROTC Honor Unit with Distinction, able to nominate cadets to the U.S. service academies. The corps draws roughly 75% domestic cadets from many states and about 25% international cadets.
college_matriculation: Graduates achieve essentially 100% college acceptance, including appointments to the U.S. service academies (West Point, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, Coast Guard Academy).
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS)
notable_alumni: Maxie Anderson (balloonist), Clifton Cates (19th Commandant of the U.S. Marine Corps), Dale Dye (actor/military advisor), Howard Hughes Sr. (industrialist)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid is available; international cadets pay additional mandatory, uniform, and international fees.
head_of_school: Brigadier General Richard V. Geraci, USA (Ret.) (President)
athletic_conference: Missouri State High School Activities Association (Class 1)

name: Thomas Jefferson School
about: Thomas Jefferson School is a small coeducational, non-sectarian, independent boarding and day school in suburban St. Louis (Sunset Hills), Missouri, founded in 1946. It serves an intentionally small, academically talented group of students in grades 7-12, with strong emphasis on rigorous college-preparatory academics and small classes. Roughly 75 students enroll, about 17% of them international.
college_matriculation: 100% of graduates enroll in college; the school reports strong placement at selective colleges and universities.
